title: Admin Folder Delete Ability (Bug/Feature?)
excerpt:
I’m not sure if this is a bug or a feature, but when you’re an admin, even with highest permissions, you cannot delete a folder if someone else created it. Even lower permission users. 
 
The best an admin can do is rena…

posts.author: Aaren (Aaren)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m not sure if this is a bug or a feature, but when you’re an admin, even with highest permissions, you cannot delete a folder if someone else created it. Even lower permission users.

The best an admin can do is rename the folder with something like “Delete Me”. Since it’s not a board, it does not contain a log of who the creator was, which means finding them to request deletion can be difficult.

I’m unsure if this is a bug or a feature, but the ability to delete/alter pretty much any thing is essential for those with admin/owner rights so they can moderate the system appropriately.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think this issue happens when in this folder are a board that you have not created.
We have seen the same issue, and i dont know how to solve this.

posts.author: Noa-monday.com (Noa )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Aaren, thanks for writing in!
When deleting the boards inside, the folder should not be displayed anymore so this looks like a bug. Could you let me us know if you’re still experiencing the issue. And to rule out all possibilities, is it possible there are Private/Shareable boards inside the folder that you don’t have access to?

posts.author: Aaren (Aaren)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Noa — I had the user delete the folder so it’s not an issue for us anymore, but it can still happen.

I suppose it’s possible there’s a private board, but as an admin I have no way within Monday to confirm or disprove that unless I go ask all of our employees, which isn’t realistic. If admins had the ability to see but not access any private board, that’d definitely help.

Thanks!

posts.author: GUM (Ryan at GUM)
posts.cookedHtml: As an admin-level user, I moved a board from one folder to another and yet am unable to delete the now empty folder because I did not create it. Echoing the above, as an admin user, it is critical I am able to override the default permissions/ creations of lower-level users. Please help!

posts.author: bmw (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml: I just experienced this same issue - as an Admin I can’t delete a folder that a user created, even when there are no visible boards in the folder. Please fix.

posts.author: adminseb (Seb M.)
posts.cookedHtml: @Noa-monday.com can we please make this a priority? It is very, really important for us that admins are able to delete folders. This is holding us up massively as we have one person who accidentally created many many folders and is not around to delete them all! It’s really messing us up!

posts.author: Morfix (Henry Spierer)
posts.cookedHtml: Frustrating. We’re a small company and have few users, and one user was removed. Now I’m stuck with his obsolete folders cluttering up my workspace. I shudder to think what this would do to mid-large sized company admins.

posts.author: dcarroll1650 (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: Was there ever a fix on this? My company just joined Monday and when C suite executives create folders and we need to delete them we can’t be hounding our C suite to be making time in their schedule to delete folders.

posts.author: LucaDK (Luca Carraro)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Noa-monday.com I’m still experiencing the same problem. Is there any way to solve this?
Thanks

posts.author: c2tina (Tina Frost)
posts.cookedHtml: This seems like a common sense feature that admins/owners can see and delete whatever they need to from within their workspace.

posts.author: AverageMondayUser (Aaron)
posts.cookedHtml: Can this please be fixed? It’s extremely annoying.

posts.author: JATTHT (Julia Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello - I am having the same issue in our main workspace. Is there a fix yet?

posts.author: mdeslauriers (Michèle DesLauriers)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here! Any fixes yet?

posts.author: ghonn (Gretchen Honnold)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also having this challenge and am hoping for a fix!

posts.author: hwiner (Hal Winer)
posts.cookedHtml: same issue … sept 23. updates?

posts.author: SupMarioFro (Mario Marxen)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! I’d like to bring this topic up again. Maybe there is a solution already which I am not aware off. Anyways, at the moment it (still) seems like that only the creator of a folder will be able to delete it. That makes it quite difficult, if not impossible, to keep the workspace clean. Can you please share the latest status? I have found the opportunity to delete a folder via an API, but this is not applicable for our use case. Thank you! Mario

posts.author: kylechlopcik (Kyle Chlopcik)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? We have several folders created by person who left the company, unable to clean our workspace.

posts.author: dnmonday (Desmond)
posts.cookedHtml: Not an ideal solution by any means, but I was able to create my own folder, move the undeletable folder(s) inside, and then delete everything when deleting the parent folder that I created.

posts.author: Mallory (Mallory Napier)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m experiencing this issue now. The work around to put in a folder I create solved my issue, but this really seems like it should not be required.

posts.author: KLS13 (Kirsty)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Is there an update on this missing feature? It seems wanted from the community and has been updateless for 4 years.

posts.author: krasten (Kristen Harris)
posts.cookedHtml: Still looking for a fix for this! Admins should be able to delete.

posts.author: benmahlermining (Ben Mahler)
posts.cookedHtml: 4 and a half years later, this is still the case. I would say bug, not feature.

posts.author: Jodi (Jodi Dorfler)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Monday - it’s been 4 years since the OP inquired about this bug. It’s still an issue so please put this on top of your priority list for fixes.

posts.author: briann (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml: Create a new folder and move the folder you wanted to delete inside. Then delete the newly created folder. It should take care of them both.
